# Review: `deepmind/981.lean` ‚Äî Erd≈ës Problem 981

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd≈ës Problem 981`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source.
- [ ] **FAIL**: Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem. The module docstring has the reference and a bibliography entry for Elliott [El69], but does not include any description of what the problem actually asks (the asymptotic formula for sums of $f_\varepsilon(p)$). The problem description appears only in the theorem docstring. A one- or two-sentence summary should be added to the module docstring.
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[El69]` for Elliott (1970).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos981 ... end Erdos981`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos981`.
- [x] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block: `open Filter` and `open scoped Topology Real` on lines 28‚Äì30.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `legendrePartialSum` has a LaTeX docstring.
- [x] `fEpsilon` has a LaTeX docstring.
- [x] `fEpsilon` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf` over a classical set).
- [x] `legendrePartialSum` is not marked `noncomputable` (computable ‚Äî it uses `Finset.sum` and `jacobiSym`, both decidable). Correct.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Function names use `lowerCamelCase`: `legendrePartialSum`, `fEpsilon`.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_981` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the same attribute line: `@[category research solved, AMS 11]`.
- [x] Theorem has a docstring referencing `[El69]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem says "Prove that ‚àë_{p<x} f_Œµ(p) ~ c_Œµ ¬∑ x/log x" ‚Äî a direct assertion (**Case 3**).

- [x] Case 3 correctly applied: theorem type is `‚àÉ c : ‚Ñù, c > 0 ‚àß Tendsto ...` directly, with no `answer()` wrapper.
- [x] No quantifiers incorrectly placed as theorem parameters. `Œµ` is a parameter (not quantified inside `answer()`), which is correct because this is Case 3 ‚Äî the `answer()` parameter rule only applies to Cases 1 and 2.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research solved`.
- [x] Value is valid: `research solved` (Elliott proved this in 1970).
- [x] Not `research open`, so the `sorry` consistency check is N/A.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 11`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `11` (Number theory).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single entry ‚Äî trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line format, acceptable per checklist ¬ß9).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the solved problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

N/A ‚Äî no variants present.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚Ñù`, `‚Ñ§`, `‚Ñï`, `‚àÄ`, `‚àÉ`, `‚â•`, `‚àß`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (<100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] Not verified (requires `lake build`).

---

## Summary

**Result: 1 issue found.**

| # | Severity | Item | Detail |
|---|----------|------|--------|
| 1 | Minor | ¬ß3 Module Docstring | Missing plain-English problem description. The module docstring contains only the reference link and bibliography entry, but no summary of what the problem asks. Add something like: *"For every Œµ > 0, is it true that ‚àë_{p<x} f_Œµ(p) ~ c_Œµ ¬∑ x/(log x) for some constant c_Œµ > 0, where f_Œµ(p) is the smallest m such that ‚àë_{n‚â§N} (n/p) < ŒµN for all N ‚â• m? Proved by Elliott [El69]."* |

The formalization is otherwise clean and well-structured. The definitions `legendrePartialSum` and `fEpsilon` correctly capture the mathematical objects, the asymptotic statement using `Tendsto` and `nhds c` is a reasonable encoding of "~", and the `@[category research solved, AMS 11]` metadata is appropriate.

    19‚Üí/-!

    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/981](https://www.erdosproblems.com/981)
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí[El69] Elliott, P. D. T. A., _On the mean value of $f(p)$_. Proc. London Math. Soc. (3) 21
    25‚Üí(1970), 28‚Äì96.

    30‚Üíopen scoped Topology Real

    37‚Üí  (Finset.Icc 1 N).sum (fun n => jacobiSym (n : ‚Ñ§) p)
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/-- $f_\varepsilon(p)$: the smallest positive integer $m$ such that for all $N \ge m$,
    40‚Üí    $\sum_{n \le N} (n/p) < \varepsilon \cdot N$. Returns $0$ if no such $m$ exists. -/
    41‚Üínoncomputable def fEpsilon (Œµ : ‚Ñù) (p : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
    42‚Üí  sInf {m : ‚Ñï | m ‚â• 1 ‚àß ‚àÄ N : ‚Ñï, N ‚â• m ‚Üí (legendrePartialSum p N : ‚Ñù) < Œµ * (N : ‚Ñù)}
    43‚Üí

    45‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 981 (proved by Elliott [El69]):

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 981

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/981](https://www.erdosproblems.com/981)

[El69] Elliott, P. D. T. A., _On the mean value of $f(p)$_. Proc. London Math. Soc. (3) 21
(1970), 28‚Äì96.
-/

open Filter

open scoped Topology Real

namespace Erdos981

/-- The partial sum of Legendre symbols $(n/p)$ for $n = 1, \ldots, N$.
    We use the Jacobi symbol which coincides with the Legendre symbol for prime $p$. -/
def legendrePartialSum (p : ‚Ñï) (N : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñ§ :=
  (Finset.Icc 1 N).sum (fun n => jacobiSym (n : ‚Ñ§) p)

/-- $f_\varepsilon(p)$: the smallest positive integer $m$ such that for all $N \ge m$,
    $\sum_{n \le N} (n/p) < \varepsilon \cdot N$. Returns $0$ if no such $m$ exists. -/
noncomputable def fEpsilon (Œµ : ‚Ñù) (p : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
  sInf {m : ‚Ñï | m ‚â• 1 ‚àß ‚àÄ N : ‚Ñï, N ‚â• m ‚Üí (legendrePartialSum p N : ‚Ñù) < Œµ * (N : ‚Ñù)}

/--
Erd≈ës Problem 981 (proved by Elliott [El69]):

For every $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $c_\varepsilon > 0$ such that
$$\sum_{\substack{p < x \\ p \text{ prime}}} f_\varepsilon(p) \sim c_\varepsilon \cdot
\frac{x}{\log x}.$$

Formulated as: the ratio $\sum_{p < x} f_\varepsilon(p) / (x / \log x)$ tends to
$c_\varepsilon$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_981 (Œµ : ‚Ñù) (hŒµ : Œµ > 0) :
    ‚àÉ c : ‚Ñù, c > 0 ‚àß
      Tendsto
        (fun x : ‚Ñï =>
          (((Finset.range x).filter Nat.Prime).sum (fun p => fEpsilon Œµ p) : ‚Ñù) /
            ((x : ‚Ñù) / Real.log (x : ‚Ñù)))
        atTop (nhds c) := by
  sorry

end Erdos981

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/981](https://www.erdosproblems.com/981)

For every Œµ > 0, let f_Œµ(p) be the smallest m such that ‚àë_{n‚â§N} (n/p) < ŒµN for all N ‚â• m.
Is it true that ‚àë_{p<x} f_Œµ(p) ~ c_Œµ ¬∑ x/(log x) for some constant c_Œµ > 0?
Proved by Elliott [El69].

[El69]

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 981

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/981](https://www.erdosproblems.com/981)

[El69] Elliott, P. D. T. A., _On the mean value of $f(p)$_. Proc. London Math. Soc. (3) 21
(1970), 28‚Äì96.
-/

open Filter

open scoped Topology Real

namespace Erdos981

/-- The partial sum of Legendre symbols $(n/p)$ for $n = 1, \ldots, N$.
    We use the Jacobi symbol which coincides with the Legendre symbol for prime $p$. -/
def legendrePartialSum (p : ‚Ñï) (N : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñ§ :=
  (Finset.Icc 1 N).sum (fun n => jacobiSym (n : ‚Ñ§) p)

/-- $f_\varepsilon(p)$: the smallest positive integer $m$ such that for all $N \ge m$,
    $\sum_{n \le N} (n/p) < \varepsilon \cdot N$. Returns $0$ if no such $m$ exists. -/
noncomputable def fEpsilon (Œµ : ‚Ñù) (p : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
  sInf {m : ‚Ñï | m ‚â• 1 ‚àß ‚àÄ N : ‚Ñï, N ‚â• m ‚Üí (legendrePartialSum p N : ‚Ñù) < Œµ * (N : ‚Ñù)}

/--
Erd≈ës Problem 981 (proved by Elliott [El69]):

For every $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $c_\varepsilon > 0$ such that
$$\sum_{\substack{p < x \\ p \text{ prime}}} f_\varepsilon(p) \sim c_\varepsilon \cdot
\frac{x}{\log x}.$$

Formulated as: the ratio $\sum_{p < x} f_\varepsilon(p) / (x / \log x)$ tends to
$c_\varepsilon$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_981 (Œµ : ‚Ñù) (hŒµ : Œµ > 0) :
    ‚àÉ c : ‚Ñù, c > 0 ‚àß
      Tendsto
        (fun x : ‚Ñï =>
          (((Finset.range x).filter Nat.Prime).sum (fun p => fEpsilon Œµ p) : ‚Ñù) /
            ((x : ‚Ñù) / Real.log (x : ‚Ñù)))
        atTop (nhds c) := by
  sorry

end Erdos981
